title: helianthemum glaucum var. stoechadifolium
intro: Helianthemum glaucum var. stoechadifolium, also known as Rockrose, is a plant species within the Helianthemum genus, characterized by its grayish-green foliage and small pink flowers. Traditionally, Rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and promoting wound healing due to its ability to stimulate the production of collagen and improve tissue repair. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
